things.name: Estadio de la Cerámica
things.description: Compact and atmospheric 23,500-capacity home of Villarreal CF, the Yellow Submarine, one of Spain's most charming grounds

things.name: Villarreal City Centre
things.description: Compact Valencian city with a pleasant town hall square, local tapas bars and the civic pride of a great footballing town

things.name: Basílica de la Virgen de Gracia
things.description: Neoclassical basilica housing the beloved patroness of Villarreal, the focus of the city's most important annual festival

things.name: Museu de la Rajoleria
things.description: Fascinating ceramic tile museum celebrating Villarreal's historic tile-making industry that gave the stadium its name

things.name: Castellón Old Town
things.description: The regional capital Castellón, just 8km from Villarreal, with a Gothic cathedral, medieval old quarter and vibrant tapas scene

things.name: Planetari de Castelló
things.description: Modern planetarium in Castellón with full-dome astronomical shows and exhibitions on the universe and space exploration

things.name: Playa de Almassora
things.description: Long sandy Mediterranean beach with calm waters just a short drive from Villarreal, perfect for a pre or post-match swim

things.name: Parque de la Ciudad
things.description: The main public park of Villarreal with gardens, a lake, children's play areas and a pleasant café terrace

things.name: Peñíscola Castle
things.description: Dramatic Templar castle on a rock jutting into the Mediterranean, the fortress of antipope Benedict XIII, known as the City in the Sea

things.name: Museu de Belles Arts de Castelló
things.description: Provincial fine arts museum in Castellón with prehistoric, archaeological and painting collections spanning the region's history
